name: Grilled Rosemary Lamb Chops
recipeIngredient: 3 Tablespoons minced fresh rosemary, or 3 teaspoons dried
12 Each 1-inch Thick Loin Lamb Chops, fat trimmed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, lemon juice, minced fresh rosemary (or dried rosemary), minced garlic, and ground black pepper.
2. Place the lamb chops in a single layer in a 13x9x2-inch glass dish.
3. Pour the marinade over the lamb chops, ensuring they are evenly coated.
4. Cover the dish and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, turning occasionally to allow the flavors to meld.
5. Prepare the barbecue with medium-high heat.
6. When the coals turn white (if using charcoal), drain any excess ash and scatter wood chips over the coals if desired. If using wood chips, let them begin to smoke.
7. Season the lamb chops with salt and pepper.
8. Cover the grill and cook the lamb chops for about 4 minutes per side, or until they reach the desired doneness (medium-rare).
9. Serve and enjoy.
4 Servings Bon Appitit July 1995
NOTES: Up to 1 1/2 cups mesquite wood chips, soaked in cold water for 1 hour, may be used to grill chops.
